note: seq=84: FIX the Meta Guardian v2 publish-staleness. After seq-83 the guardian feed went stale (no guardian-latest/meta-guardian-status refresh) -- most likely the thorough v2 --once pass (every unit + PG + many freshness checks) exceeded the tight 50s TimeoutStartSec at 45s cadence and got SIGKILLed before publishing. This raises TimeoutStartSec 50->120 and relaxes cadence 45->60s so a full pass completes + publishes, and runs the first pass BLOCKING (capture rc) so a non-zero exit surfaces in seq-84-ok detail instead of silently failing. If it still doesn't publish, the --once is crashing (needs box logs). Detection latency target ~60s.

steps.note: seq=84: guardian timeout 120s + 60s cadence so the v2 pass completes and publishes.

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS Meta Guardian v2 (fast supervisory self-heal + predictive watch)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Nice=5
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/autopull.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/audit.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/guardian/meta_guardian.py --once
TimeoutStartSec=120

[Unit]
Description=ATLAS Meta Guardian v2 timer (60s cadence)

[Timer]
OnBootSec=20s
OnUnitActiveSec=60s
AccuracySec=5s
Unit=atlas-guardian.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import os,subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','daemon-reload']); s.run(['systemctl','enable','atlas-guardian.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','restart','atlas-guardian.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-guardian.service']); r=s.run(['systemctl','start','atlas-guardian.service'],capture_output=True,text=True); print('guardian start rc',r.returncode, (r.stderr or '')[-200:])"
